steps.title: Set up the coverage rate
steps.content: One gallon covers 170 square feet. Therefore, to cover an area of $A$ square feet, we need $S = \frac{A}{170}$ gallons.

steps.title: Account for staining twice
steps.content:
Since we stain the fence twice, the total area covered is $2w$ square feet. Substitute this into our rate equation:
$$S = \frac{2w}{170}$$

steps.title: Simplify the fraction
steps.content:
Simplify by dividing both numerator and denominator by 2:
$$S = \frac{2w}{170} = \frac{w}{85}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
